name: Muggine in Bianco
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Homemade mayonnaise made with olive oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Fish must be absolutely fresh. Clean the fish well, ensuring the gills are removed from the head.
2. Separate the head from the body and remove all bones from the fish. Save the head and bones.
3. Place onion, carrot, celery, one slice of lemon, and peppercorns into a fish poacher.
4. Add the fish filets, head, and bones to the poacher.
5. Add cold water to cover and 1/2 tsp salt. Simmer, covered, for 10-20 minutes or until done.
6. The fish is done when the eye pops out slightly and the meat flakes easily.
7. Remove from heat. Carefully pick out any pieces of meat, avoiding bones and vegetables, and arrange inside a fish mold or on an oval serving plate.
8. Season with salt, white pepper, and lemon juice.
9. Strain the broth and return to the stove. Let it boil uncovered until the liquid is reduced to about a cup.
10. Pour the reduced broth over the fish and refrigerate until the gelatin is firm.
11. To serve, unmold and cut the fish in half lengthwise with a sharp knife.
12. Cut each half into four or five pieces diagonally, forming a fishbone pattern.
13. Mask the cuts under a swirl of mayonnaise so the effect looks like a whole fish.
14. Cut lemon slices and arrange around the fish to look like fins.
